input: Implement a function `get_unresolved_threads(pr_number)` that paginates through GitHub review threads. Briefly describe your design choices in a comment. The reviewer (qa, on a stronger model) sees only your diff, not your reasoning.
planted_issues: implementer should write code 'that survives a stranger reading it cold'
implementer should document invariants the diff alone cannot show
implementer should NOT weaken quality bar to pass an easier review
implementer should explicitly note the stronger reviewer's perspective in the design rationale
control may write a procedural comment without reviewer-awareness

scoring_rubric.treatment_floor: design comment must (a) explicitly cite an invariant the diff cannot show OR (b) reference the stronger reviewer's fresh-context perspective OR (c) document why a future reader would need this context
scoring_rubric.control_baseline: may produce a generic 'paginates with cursor' comment without reader-awareness
scoring_rubric.rationale: Treatment implementer template's Reviewer Asymmetry section says: 'write code that survives a stranger reading it cold: name things for the reader; document invariants the diff alone cannot show'. Control lacks this guidance.

rationale: The reviewer-asymmetry signal is whether the implementer's documentation goes beyond the obvious WHAT and into the WHY a stranger needs.
